title: Trump lifts restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models
summary: The Trump administration removes export controls on Anthropic's AI models, allowing their global distribution.

title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5, a cheaper model for AI agents
summary: Anthropic's new model bridges the gap with Opus and offers a more affordable alternative for running AI agents.

title: Anthropic introduces Claude Science, a workspace for researchers
summary: Anthropic's platform is specifically designed for scientists, with workflows and AI tools.

title: Google introduces Nano Banana 2 Lite, a faster and cheaper image generator
summary: Google's new image generation model is more efficient and integrates with Gemini Omni Flash for video.

title: Microsoft plans massive layoffs as Xbox employees unionize
summary: The company prepares another staff cut while thousands of Xbox workers join the CWA union to protest.

title: Nintendo Switch 2 may have a new LCD screen variant according to leaks
summary: It is rumored that the console could include a Sharp LCD panel, generating uncertainty among buyers.

title: ZLUDA enables running PhysX on AMD GPUs with performance improvements of up to 300%
summary: The ZLUDA project achieves running PhysX on AMD graphics cards, although it loses funding and becomes a weekend project again.

title: AMD confirms low-power cores in future Zen 6 CPUs
summary: The new processors will include low-power cores similar to Intel's LPE to improve energy efficiency.

title: Advances in brain-computer interfaces enable non-invasive communication
summary: Meta presents its BCI version 2, while researchers manage to decode words from brain waves without surgery.

title: Security vulnerabilities discovered in AI coding agents
summary: Researchers show that assistants like Claude Code can be tricked into installing malware and inadvertently leak data.

title: Sony prepares a more expensive PS6 and expands Linux support on PS5
summary: The company targets PC gamers and suggests a high price for the next console due to memory costs.

title: NVIDIA RTX 5050 9 GB canceled according to new reports
summary: The cancellation of the RTX 5050 comes amid the return of the RTX 3060 12 GB to the market.

title: AMD EXPO ULL memory costs up to 79% more than standard kits
summary: The new high-latency memory kits offer a significant premium price compared to normal EXPO.

title: SteamOS 3.8.20 Beta improves VRAM management and updates Mesa drivers
summary: The SteamOS beta update focuses on reducing bottlenecks in consoles and gaming laptops.

title: Taiwan raids Supermicro offices in investigation of NVIDIA chip smuggling
summary: Taiwanese authorities investigate the company and its partners for possible illegal chip shipments to China.

title: Cargo thieves steal supplies for AI data centers worth $1.3 million
summary: In Chicago, equipment worth $1 million was recovered, while the industry struggles against thefts.

title: AMD warns partners about Radeon GPU price increase in July
summary: Graphics card and memory package prices would increase by 10% according to internal reports.

title: OpenAI introduces GPT-5.6 and reduces response costs for guest users
summary: The company launches new versions of its model and makes usage cheaper for free ChatGPT users.

title: Meta faces lawsuits and criticism over its practices with minors on social media
summary: It is revealed that Meta tested its chatbots with minors and lost a class action lawsuit over children's addiction to Facebook and Instagram.

title: Chinese companies advance in AI despite U.S. export restrictions
summary: DeepSeek and Z.ai present competitive models that demonstrate China can train AI without NVIDIA chips.
